pokemon:gligar.name: Gligar
pokemon:gligar.summary: Gligar glides through the air without a sound as if it were sliding. This Pokémon hangs on to the face of its foe using its clawed hind legs and the large pincers on its forelegs, then injects the prey with its poison barb.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Gligar glides through the air without a sound as if it were sliding. This Pokémon hangs on to the face of its foe using its clawed hind legs and the large pincers on its forelegs, then injects the prey with its poison barb.
It glides as if sliding. It startles foes by clamping on to their faces, then jabs with its poison stinger.
It clamps on to its chosen prey then jabs the stinger on its tail into the prey while it’s stunned with surprise.
It builds its nest on a steep cliff. When it is done gliding, it hops along the ground back to its nest.
It sails on the winds with its limbs extended to strike from the sky. It aims for the prey’s face.
It glides without making a single sound. It grasps the face of its foe using its hind and large front claws, then stabs with its poison barb.
It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.
It flies straight at its target's face then clamps down on the star­ tled victim to inject poison.
It flies straight at its target’s face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.
It flies straight at its target’s face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

Its tail is tipped by a thick, horrible stinger. To bring down prey, it will first obscure their vision by covering their faces with its body, and then it will use the stinger to inject them with venom.

Gligar glides through the air without a sound as if it were sliding. This Pokémon hangs on to the face of its foe using its clawed hind legs and the large pincers on its forelegs, then injects the prey with its poison barb.
It sails on the winds with its limbs extended to strike from the sky. It aims for the prey’s face.
It glides as if sliding. It startles foes by clamping on to their faces, then jabs with its poison stinger.

GLIGAR glides through the air without a sound as if it were sliding. This POKéMON hangs on to the face of its foe using its clawed hind legs and the large pincers on its forelegs, then injects the prey with its poison barb.

It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

It clamps on to its chosen prey then jabs the stinger on its tail into the prey while it’s stunned with surprise.
It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.
It flies straight at its target’s face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

pokemon:gliscor.name: Gliscor
pokemon:gliscor.summary: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey... Then its elongated fangs do the rest.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey... Then its elongated fangs do the rest.
If it succeeds in catching even a faint breeze properly, it can circle the globe without flapping once.
It dances silently through the sky. When it approaches prey, it can land a critical hit in an instant.
It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!
Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey... Then its elongated fangs do the rest.
It glides soundlessly on pitch-black wings and sinks sharp fangs into the throat of its prey. It takes on a look of satisfaction once it has entirely drained its prey of blood.
It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!
It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!
